content: The orthographic variant spelling 'reflexion' (the older, less common alternative to 'reflection' that carries both linguistic and educational meaning in high school English curricula) can be repurposed as a naming convention and conceptual framing for a new LLM self-reflection mechanism that explicitly splits reflection into two distinct stages: 're-' (revisiting prior outputs) and '-flexion' (physical bending, metaphorically framed as flexing the model's reasoning space to bend away from incorrect default paths instead of just mirroring existing outputs).
